According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}

'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.qq63/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />

'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}
== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]][[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}
== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}
==References== <references group="lower-alpha" />{{reflist}} {{Miwa and Yamato Faith}}

He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}
== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

[[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Saonetsuhiko-->[[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Saonetsuhiko-->[[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:Unsorted]][[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category: Completion categories]] <!--Saonetsuhiko-->[[Category:kk]][[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:Saonetsuhiko]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] [[simple:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] {{User:Immanuelle/Furutama Faith}} {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Saonetsuhiko]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]][[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}
== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}
== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]

When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>

The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]][[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}}== See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]][[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

[[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:aonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category:tier 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Saonetsuhiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:Saonetsuhiko]] [[Category:New]] {{⚠️🚨COPIED from EverybodyWiki ❗❕⚠️😡😤Please respect Licence CC-BY-SA ❗}} {{Source Wikipedia}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:completion tier 2]][[Category: tier 3]][[Category: tier 3]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:New]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 椎根津彦 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = | 時代 = 上古 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 槁根津日子 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[倭国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[倭氏]]、[[久比岐国造]]、[[明石国造]]等祖 | 父母 = [[武位起命]] | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = [[国津神]]と自称 }} [[File:Shinetsuhiko.jpg|thumb|220px|椎根津彦 ／『[[前賢故実]]』より]] '''椎根津彦'''（しいねつひこ、『[[日本書紀]]』）、'''槁根津日子'''（さおねつひこ、『[[古事記]]』）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。[[神武東征]]において登場する[[倭国造]]（倭直部）の祖、または[[大倭国造]]、大倭直の始祖。珍彦（うづひこ、『日本書紀』）あるいは宇豆彦（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。神知津彦（かむしりつひこ、『新撰姓氏録』）とも<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、pp.85-95</ref><ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 本文篇』吉川弘文館1981年、p.251</ref>。
== 概要 == [[神武天皇]]が東征において[[速吸門]]で出会った[[国津神]]で、船路の先導者となる。このとき、『日本書紀』では曲浦（わだのうら）で魚釣するところを[[シイ|椎]]の棹を授けて御船に引き入れて名を珍彦（うづひこ）から椎根津彦に改めさせたとあり、『古事記』では[[カメ|亀]]の[[甲羅]]の上に乗っていたのを棹をさし渡し御船に引き入れて槁根津日子の名を賜ったという。
『日本書紀』ではその後、神武天皇に献策して[[兄磯城]]を挟み撃ちにより破る。また神武天皇の即位後に褒賞として[[倭国造]]に任命された。
速吸門については諸説ある。『日本書紀』では[[豊予海峡]]を指すと考えられており、[[大分県]][[大分市]][[佐賀関]]には、椎根津彦を祀る椎根津彦神社がある。『古事記』では[[吉備国]]の[[児島湾]]口を指すと考えられる。[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]東区水門町には、珍彦（宇豆毘古命、うづひこのみこと）の乗った大亀の化身とされる亀岩を祀る[[亀石まつり|亀石神社]]（かめいわじんじゃ）がある。あるいは『古事記』が吉備の高島宮から浪速に行く間に速吸門を通ったとある点から、これを[[明石海峡]]とする考え方もある<ref>『日本古典文学大系 67 日本書紀・上』1967年、岩波書店、p.190、頭註</ref>。『日本書紀』神代上、第五段の一書第十に「乃往見 粟門及速吸名門 然此二門 潮既太急」とあり、「粟門」が[[淡路国]]と[[阿波国]]との間の海峡ならば、それに並ぶ速吸門は明石海峡となり、古事記のほうが正しいようである<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、p.92</ref>。『阿波志』によると、速吸名門は[[鳴門海峡]]<ref>『阿波志』巻之三</ref>。
また、椎根津彦命を祭神とする神社には、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[東灘区]][[本山町 (神戸市)|本山町]]の[[保久良神社]]がある。保久良神社由緒書によると「社名の起因も 1、椎根津彦命の子孫たる倉人水守等が祖先を祭祀し奉る 2、三韓役の戦利武器を収蔵するより」とあり、神武東征時速吸之門（[[明石海峡]]）に現れて軍勢を先導したとある。
椎根津彦命は保久良神社の南に位置する神戸市東灘区の[[青木 (神戸市)|青木]]（おうぎ）の浜に青亀（おうぎ）の背にのってこの浜に漂着したという伝承があり、それが青木（おうぎ）の地名の由来となった<ref>『神戸の神社 市内310社案内』2000年、兵庫県[[神社庁]]神戸市支部 編著</ref>。
吉井良隆は保久良神社について「椎根津彦命は[[大阪湾]]北側を支配する海部の首長であったとされ、西宮夷（兵庫県[[西宮市]][[西宮神社]]）の奥夷社の元宮」と推測している<ref>『日本の神々―神社と聖地―3 摂津・河内・和泉・淡路』2000年、[[谷川健一]]、[[大和岩雄]]、[[落合重信]]、河尻正、小林章、武田信一、田村利久、東瀬博司、濱岡きみ子、林利喜雄、古田実、松下煌、松本翠耕 編著、[[白水社]]</ref>。 [[ファイル:YamatoSukune.jpg|サムネイル|293x293ピクセル|籠神社の倭宿禰]]
== 異説 == 丹後半島の[[籠神社]]（このじんじゃ）には「別名・'''珍彦'''・'''椎根津彦'''・'''神知津彦''' 籠宮主祭神天孫[[彦火明命]]第四代 海部宮司家四代目の祖 [[神武東征]]の途次、[[明石海峡]]（[[速吸門]]）に亀に乗って現れ、[[神武天皇]]を先導して浪速、河内、大和へと進み、幾多の献策に依り大和建国の第一の功労者として、[[神武天皇]]から'''倭宿禰'''（やまとすくね）の称号を賜る。外に大倭国造、倭直とも云う。」とあり、境内には亀の背に乗った'''倭宿禰'''の像がある。
== 系譜 == [[椎根津彦神社]]の伝承によれば、父は[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]の子・[[武位起命]]で、妹には[[祥持姫命]]がいるとされる。 『旧事紀』国造本紀には椎根津彦を彦火々出見尊の孫とする異伝が記されており、皇孫本紀には椎根津彦の祖父は彦火々出見尊、父は武位起命とする異伝が記されている<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、p.91</ref>。
== 後裔氏族 == *[[倭国造]] *[[久比岐国造]] - [[崇神天皇]]の御代に椎根津彦の5世孫の御戈命が任命された。 *[[明石国造]] - [[応神天皇]]の御代に椎根津彦の8世孫の都弥自宿禰が任命された。 *[[青海氏]] - 久比岐国造族の頸城直の支族。 *[[吉備海部氏]] *[[依羅物忌氏]] *[[海氏]]

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]][[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true}}}} {{complex}}[[fr:Saonetsuhiko]]
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web |title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama}}. [[ja:椎根津彦]]
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />

revision.text:
{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命|12=draft}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命|12=draft}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true|12=draft}}}} {{complex}}
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja|12=draft}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama|12=draft}}.
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

{{draft categories| [[Category:Deified Japanese people in the Kiki]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko position holders]] [[Category:Yamato clan]] [[Category:Kunitsukami]] [[Category:Unsorted]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category: Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Saonetsuhiko]] [[Category:Pages from Simple English Wikipedia]] [[Category:bump1-19]] [[Category:refreshing categories imported on 2024-01-19]] [[Category:Simple english wikipedia and everybodywiki]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:completion tier 2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] [[fr:Saonetsuhiko]] [[ja:椎根津彦]] [[sn:Saonetsuhiko]] [[fr:User:{{PAGENAME}}]] [[sn:aonetsuhiko]] [[en:Saonetsuhiko]] [[ja:Saonetsuhiko]]

{{draft}} {{Promising draft|reason=the high amount of work put into it over a long time, see edit history}} {{afc comment|1=page was originally created in the draft namespace on english wikipedia. It was then copied to userspace on simple english wikipedia and edited before being imported here. The entire simple english wikipedia history is present, but the wikipedia draft can be found on everybodywiki here [[every:Saonetsuhiko|Saonetsuhiko]] and should be undeleted here on wikipedia [[:en:draft:Saonetsuhiko]] and have the revisions imported to this wiki to give proper attribution. While most edits are by Immanuelle there might be a few from other users. [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命|12=draft}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命|12=draft}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true|12=draft}}}} {{complex}}
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja|12=draft}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama|12=draft}}.
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
==Genealogy== {{User:Immanuelle/Generations of Watatsumi}} == See Also ==
* [[Ōmiwa Shrine]] * {{ill|Miwa clan|en|12=draft}} * {{ill|Hotsuma Tsutae|en}}

[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:36, 18 February 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment}} {{Expand Japanese}} {{infobox royalty||father='''{{ill|Takeuioki no mikoto|ja|武位起命|12=draft}}'''|issue={{ill|Shimatsu no mikoto|ja|志麻津見命|12=draft}}|type=Shinto|image=Shinetsuhiko.jpg|caption=Saonetsuhiko as depicted in {{ill|Zenken Kojitsu|en}} (1903)|house=Ancestor of {{ill|Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Yamato clan|ja|倭国造|simple|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kubiki Kokuzo|ja|久比岐国造|complete=true|12=draft}} and {{ill|Akashi Kokuzo|ja|明石国造|complete=true|12=draft}}}} {{complex}}
'''Saonetsuhiko''' (椎根津彦) is a Japanese deity..<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>{{Cite journal|last=Yōko|first=Isse|date=2019|title=Revisiting Tsuda Sōkichi in Postwar Japan: “Misunderstandings” and the Historical Facts of the Kiki|url=https://www.jstor.org/stable/26864868|journal=Japan Review|issue=34|pages=139–160|issn=0915-0986}}</ref><ref>{{Cite web|title=Ancient Japan through Harima no Kuni Fudoki|url=https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/digital_museum/harimanokunifudoki/english/|access-date=2024-03-21|website=兵庫県立歴史博物館:兵庫県教育委員会|language=ja}}</ref> He was the ancestor of the [[Yamato no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Yamato clan]] and key figure in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko">{{Cite web|title=Encyclopedia of Shinto詳細 |url=https://d-museum.kokugakuin.ac.jp/eos/detail/id=9159 |access-date=2023-10-27 |website=國學院大學デジタルミュージアム |language=ja|12=draft}}</ref><ref name="gov">https://rekihaku.pref.hyogo.lg.jp/wp-content/themes/rekihaku/assets/pdf/harimanokunifudoki/english/chapter_3_5.pdf</ref> He was the first lord of the [[Yamato Province|Yamato Province]].<ref>{{cite book |chapter=Book Ten Kokuzō Hongi |series=Brill's Japanese Studies Library |date=2006 |volume=25 |doi=10.1163/9789047418191_022 |title=The Authenticity of Sendai Kuji Hongi |pages=362 |editor-last=Bentley |editor-first=John R. |publisher=BRILL |isbn=978-90-04-15225-0}}</ref> He is recognized for helping [[Emperor Jimmu|Emperor Jimmu]] navigate through the sea on his journey to the east.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />
He has alternative names including Shinetsuhiko, and Uzuhiko{{efn|These names are used in the nihon shoki}}<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> and Yamato no Sukune.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Suzuki |first=Masanobu |url=http://dx.doi.org/10.4324/9781315270852 |title=Clans and Genealogy in Ancient Japan |date=2017-02-03 |publisher=Routledge |isbn=978-1-315-27085-2 |pages=288}}</ref>
His grandfather is {{ill|Furutama|simple|User:Immanuelle/Furutama|lt=Furutama|en:draft|Furutama-no-mikoto|ja|布留多摩命|fr|Furutama|12=draft}}.
== Encounter with Emperor Jimmu == Saonetsuhiko is featured in two main Japanese historical texts, the [[Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and the [[Kojiki|Kojiki]].<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> And is mentioned in the Harima Fudoki.<ref name="gov" /> The Nihongi mentions the meeting with Jimmu right after his departure from [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]], whereas the Kojiki places it fifteen years later, after Jimmu had traveled through several regions. Both texts, however, agree on Saonetsuhiko's importance as the first spirit to offer his services to Jimmu and as an ancestor to important Yamato clans.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> [[simple:user:immanuelle/Saonetsuhiko]]
According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], Saonetsuhiko met Emperor Jimmu during the emperor's campaign to the east. The meeting took place when Jimmu was leaving [[Hyūga Province|Hyūga Province]] by ship. At the "straits of Hayasui," Jimmu saw Saonetsuhiko [[:en:Fishing|fishing]] from a turtle's back or, in another version of the story, from a small boat, and using sails or wings. When Jimmu asked for his name, Saonetsuhiko introduced himself as a {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}} with a deep understanding of the sea routes. This was the first use of the term which refers to local earth gods in Shinto. He offered his services to Jimmu, who then pulled Saonetsuhiko into his ship using the tip of a pole.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This was the first usage of the term {{ill|kunitsukami|en|4=fr|12=draft}}.<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /> This act led to his name Saonetsuhiko, which means "pole-root-man."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" /><ref name="gov" /><ref>https://nichibun.repo.nii.ac.jp/record/7222/files/jare_032_005.pdf</ref>
Saonetsuhiko played a major role in {{ill|Jimmu's Eastern Expedition|en}}. He observed and reported on a magical divination that Jimmu performed to defeat a group of eighty bandits in Shiki of Yamato. This divination involved collecting earth from the Heavenly Mount Kagu for the ritual. Saonetsuhiko, and a group of special forces infiltrated enemy lines to collect this earth. Furthermore, Saonetsuhiko was a military strategist, he used a "weak army" as a decoy to attract the enemy's forces, then defeating them with a "strong army."<ref name="Saonetsuhiko" />

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 椎根津彦 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = 220px | 画像説明 = | 時代 = 上古 | 生誕 = 不詳 | 死没 = 不詳 | 改名 = | 別名 = 槁根津日子 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[倭国造]] | 主君 = [[神武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[倭氏]]、[[久比岐国造]]、[[明石国造]]等祖 | 父母 = [[武位起命]] | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = [[国津神]]と自称 }} [[File:Shinetsuhiko.jpg|thumb|220px|椎根津彦 ／『[[前賢故実]]』より]] '''椎根津彦'''（しいねつひこ、『[[日本書紀]]』）、'''槁根津日子'''（さおねつひこ、『[[古事記]]』）は、[[日本神話]]に登場する[[神 (神道)|神]]。[[神武東征]]において登場する[[倭国造]]（倭直部）の祖、または[[大倭国造]]、大倭直の始祖。珍彦（うづひこ、『日本書紀』）あるいは宇豆彦（『[[新撰姓氏録]]』）。神知津彦（かむしりつひこ、『新撰姓氏録』）とも<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、pp.85-95</ref><ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 本文篇』吉川弘文館1981年、p.251</ref>。
== 概要 == [[神武天皇]]が東征において[[速吸門]]で出会った[[国津神]]で、船路の先導者となる。このとき、『日本書紀』では曲浦（わだのうら）で魚釣するところを[[シイ|椎]]の棹を授けて御船に引き入れて名を珍彦（うづひこ）から椎根津彦に改めさせたとあり、『古事記』では[[カメ|亀]]の[[甲羅]]の上に乗っていたのを棹をさし渡し御船に引き入れて槁根津日子の名を賜ったという。
『日本書紀』ではその後、神武天皇に献策して[[兄磯城]]を挟み撃ちにより破る。また神武天皇の即位後に褒賞として[[倭国造]]に任命された。
速吸門については諸説ある。『日本書紀』では[[豊予海峡]]を指すと考えられており、[[大分県]][[大分市]][[佐賀関]]には、椎根津彦を祀る椎根津彦神社がある。『古事記』では[[吉備国]]の[[児島湾]]口を指すと考えられる。[[岡山県]][[岡山市]]東区水門町には、珍彦（宇豆毘古命、うづひこのみこと）の乗った大亀の化身とされる亀岩を祀る[[亀石まつり|亀石神社]]（かめいわじんじゃ）がある。あるいは『古事記』が吉備の高島宮から浪速に行く間に速吸門を通ったとある点から、これを[[明石海峡]]とする考え方もある<ref>『日本古典文学大系 67 日本書紀・上』1967年、岩波書店、p.190、頭註</ref>。『日本書紀』神代上、第五段の一書第十に「乃往見 粟門及速吸名門 然此二門 潮既太急」とあり、「粟門」が[[淡路国]]と[[阿波国]]との間の海峡ならば、それに並ぶ速吸門は明石海峡となり、古事記のほうが正しいようである<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、p.92</ref>。『阿波志』によると、速吸名門は[[鳴門海峡]]<ref>『阿波志』巻之三</ref>。
また、椎根津彦命を祭神とする神社には、[[兵庫県]][[神戸市]][[東灘区]][[本山町 (神戸市)|本山町]]の[[保久良神社]]がある。保久良神社由緒書によると「社名の起因も 1、椎根津彦命の子孫たる倉人水守等が祖先を祭祀し奉る 2、三韓役の戦利武器を収蔵するより」とあり、神武東征時速吸之門（[[明石海峡]]）に現れて軍勢を先導したとある。
椎根津彦命は保久良神社の南に位置する神戸市東灘区の[[青木 (神戸市)|青木]]（おうぎ）の浜に青亀（おうぎ）の背にのってこの浜に漂着したという伝承があり、それが青木（おうぎ）の地名の由来となった<ref>『神戸の神社 市内310社案内』2000年、兵庫県[[神社庁]]神戸市支部 編著</ref>。
吉井良隆は保久良神社について「椎根津彦命は[[大阪湾]]北側を支配する海部の首長であったとされ、西宮夷（兵庫県[[西宮市]][[西宮神社]]）の奥夷社の元宮」と推測している<ref>『日本の神々―神社と聖地―3 摂津・河内・和泉・淡路』2000年、[[谷川健一]]、[[大和岩雄]]、[[落合重信]]、河尻正、小林章、武田信一、田村利久、東瀬博司、濱岡きみ子、林利喜雄、古田実、松下煌、松本翠耕 編著、[[白水社]]</ref>。 [[ファイル:YamatoSukune.jpg|サムネイル|293x293ピクセル|籠神社の倭宿禰]]
== 異説 == 丹後半島の[[籠神社]]（このじんじゃ）には「別名・'''珍彦'''・'''椎根津彦'''・'''神知津彦''' 籠宮主祭神天孫[[彦火明命]]第四代 海部宮司家四代目の祖 [[神武東征]]の途次、[[明石海峡]]（[[速吸門]]）に亀に乗って現れ、[[神武天皇]]を先導して浪速、河内、大和へと進み、幾多の献策に依り大和建国の第一の功労者として、[[神武天皇]]から'''倭宿禰'''（やまとすくね）の称号を賜る。外に大倭国造、倭直とも云う。」とあり、境内には亀の背に乗った'''倭宿禰'''の像がある。
== 系譜 == [[椎根津彦神社]]の伝承によれば、父は[[ホオリ|彦火火出見尊]]の子・[[武位起命]]で、妹には[[祥持姫命]]がいるとされる。 『旧事紀』国造本紀には椎根津彦を彦火々出見尊の孫とする異伝が記されており、皇孫本紀には椎根津彦の祖父は彦火々出見尊、父は武位起命とする異伝が記されている<ref>[[佐伯有清]]『新撰姓氏錄の研究 考證篇 第四』吉川弘文館1982年、p.91</ref>。
== 後裔氏族 == *[[倭国造]] *[[久比岐国造]] - [[崇神天皇]]の御代に椎根津彦の5世孫の御戈命が任命された。 *[[明石国造]] - [[応神天皇]]の御代に椎根津彦の8世孫の都弥自宿禰が任命された。 *[[青海氏]] - 久比岐国造族の頸城直の支族。 *[[吉備海部氏]] *[[依羅物忌氏]] *[[海氏]]
== 参考文献 == <references/>
== 外部リンク == * [http://www.hayasuihime.biz-web.jp/page444.html 椎根津彦神社]
== 関連項目 == *[[飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧]] *[[保久良神社]] *[[青海神社]] *[[椎根津彦神社]] *[[島津珍彦]] *[[籠神社]]
